title: Indicators | Stats NZ
content:
## Occurrence of melanoma
No trend could be determined at the 95 percent confidence level for the total age standardised rate of melanoma (per 100,000 population) between 1996 and 2013.
Sunshine hours increased at almost all selected sites throughout New Zealand from 1972 to 2016.
The ozone hole reaches its maximum annual size, and minimum ozone concentration, during the Southern Hemisphere spring.

## Cultural health index for freshwater bodies
Cultural health index scores for waterways were very good or good at 11 sites, moderate at 21 sites, and poor or very poor at nine sites, of 41 sites tested between 2005 and 2016.

## Kaitiakitanga of the Waikouaiti catchment
For Kāti Huirapa hapū, the degradation of the river and reduced ability to access mahika kai sites have a negative effect on the mauri (life force) of the river and the mana (prestige) of the rūnaka/hapū.

## Tau kōura: freshwater crayfish traditional fishing method
Anecdotal evidence suggests kōura abundance in the Te Arawa lakes has been declining, which affects important mahinga kai activities on the lakes.

## Groundwater physical stocks
In 2014, New Zealand’s aquifers held an estimated 711 billion cubic metres of groundwater.

## Selected barriers to freshwater fish in Hawke’s Bay
One-third (80) of the 240 culverts, weirs, and stormwater pump stations surveyed in the Hawke’s Bay region between 2002 and 2010 were identified as barriers to fish passage during some or all flow conditions.
In 2013, NIWA identified nine fish species, 11 invertebrate species, and 41 plant species as pests of greatest concern for New Zealand’s freshwater environments.

## Commercial coastal seabed trawling and dredging
Seabed trawling and dredging are widespread in New Zealand coastal waters.
In New Zealand waters, the number of days each year when estimated wind speeds exceed storm thresholds is highly variable. From 1979 to 2015, the southern ocean region experienced more days where storm thresholds were exceeded (5.6–17.8 days) than the north-eastern region (0.2–1.8 days).
